title: ANALYSIS OF PRODUCTION COST AND ADDED VALUE OF PALM SUGAR AGROINDUSTRY IN KEKAIT VILLAGE, GUNUNGSARI DISTRICT, WEST LOMBOK REGENCY
author: Burhanudin1, Mahyudin Nasir2, Helmi Fuadi3, Himawan Sutanto4
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This study aims to analyze the production costs and added value of the palm sugar processing agroindustry in Kekait Village, Gunungsari District, West Lombok Regency. This study uses a quantitative descriptive method using a survey method on a sample population of 36 respondents. From the total population, 10 respondents were taken purposively. The results of the study showed that the average production of palm sugar in the production process was 240 pieces with a selling price of Rp 8,000 per piece. The average production cost was Rp 767.200 , and the average income of palm sugar respondents was Rp 1.101.600, From the feasibility of the business where the efficiency value is greater than 1 (EP> 1) which is 2.5, this indicates that the palm sugar agroindustry business is feasible to run. While the Value Added Ratio (RNT) figure reached 85,34 %, this shows that if the Value Added Ratio> 50%, then the added value of the processed product is high. In the long term, to further increase production, it is important to have processing technology that can process palm sugar into refined sugar that can be consumed for processed foods and coffee.

keywords: Production Cost, Added Value, Palm Sugar Agroindustry
